evidence: var->let/const migration is essentially complete (1 var vs 137 let/const) — no old JS patterns coexisting with modern TS
The deprecated persistSourceToRegistry in source-discovery.ts is defined but has ZERO callers — dead code that can be removed
Deprecated RPC_URL and SUPERCOLONY_API constants in sdk.ts are exported but have zero importers in src/ — they exist only for backward compatibility with unknown external consumers
The 3 lifecycle.ts deprecated markers are domain-level status values ('deprecated' as a SourceStatus), not code deprecation markers — these are false positives in the migration_signals scan
All 165 source files are TypeScript — no mixed JS/TS modules
The write-rate-limit CLI migration is confirmed complete (resolved issue: CLI runners use toolkit guards, deprecated file deleted, commit 2f20e63)
Chain-first migration is complete: all 4 tools (verify, tip, scan, react) use SDK bridge chain methods, API is optional enrichment only
No stale TODO/FIXME markers referencing migrations found in src/ (only one regex pattern in response-validator.ts)
incomplete_migration.issues_preventing_higher_score: Two deprecated-but-exported symbols in sdk.ts (RPC_URL, SUPERCOLONY_API) and one deprecated function in source-discovery.ts (persistSourceToRegistry) remain as dead code. While they have no callers, they add confusion about which API to use.

incomplete_migration.dimension_character: The codebase has completed its major migrations cleanly: chain-first (API to on-chain), CLI guard migration, and JS-to-TS. The only migration residue is three deprecated symbols that are defined but never called — genuinely dead code rather than active dual-path confusion. A developer would encounter no ambiguity about which pattern to follow.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: Migrations are substantively complete with only minor dead-code residue — matching the 86-90 range. The three unused deprecated symbols prevent a higher score because they exist in the codebase without purpose, but they cause no real confusion since they have zero importers. The chain-first migration being fully complete with consistent API-fallback patterns is a strong positive.

issues.summary: Deprecated persistSourceToRegistry function has zero callers and should be removed
evidence: persistSourceToRegistry is marked @deprecated with 'Use persistSourceToCatalog for V2 catalog persistence' but grep shows zero callers in the entire codebase — it is dead code from the V1-to-V2 catalog migration
issues.suggestion: Delete the persistSourceToRegistry function (lines 324-350 approximately) and its associated V1 YAML imports if they become unused. The V2 persistSourceToCatalog function immediately below is the canonical replacement.

issues.summary: Deprecated RPC_URL and SUPERCOLONY_API constants in sdk.ts have zero importers
evidence: Both constants are marked @deprecated with 'Use getRpcUrl()/getApiUrl()' but no file in src/ imports them. They are exported dead code from before the credential-override migration.
issues.suggestion: Remove the two exported constants (lines 33-35). If external consumers might reference them, add a breaking-change note. The getter functions getRpcUrl()/getApiUrl() are the canonical replacements.

root_causes: Deprecated symbols were left in place during migrations as a safety net but never cleaned up after confirming zero callers

add.description: All toolkit tools use SDK bridge chain methods as primary path, with API as optional enrichment gated by apiAccess state. No dual-path ambiguity remains — the migration is architecturally complete, not just code-complete.

add.header: Pure TypeScript codebase with no JS residue
add.description: All 165 source files are TypeScript. var usage is down to 1 instance vs 137 let/const. No mixed JS/TS module pairs exist.

add.header: Three deprecated symbols remain as dead code
add.description: persistSourceToRegistry, RPC_URL, and SUPERCOLONY_API are deprecated and have zero callers but remain exported. These are migration cleanup items, not active confusion sources.
